feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: Does standing on a vibrating plate really burn fat and strengthen muscles?
entry.summary: Advocates sing the praises of whole-body vibration training. The science around it paints a more nuanced picture.

entry.title: Ebola Outbreak Death Toll Rises To 177: ‘Deeply Worrisome,’ WHO Chief Says (Live Updates)
entry.summary: One American who contracted Ebola is receiving treatment in Germany.

entry.title: U.S. doctor with Ebola feared he "wasn't going to make it" before evacuation from Congo
entry.summary: Dr. Peter Stafford was working with the missionary group Serge in Congo when he was infected with Ebola.

entry.title: Almost 50% of Preventable Cancers Linked to Just Two Lifestyle Habits
entry.summary: It's easy to feel powerless against cancer, but research has identified several ways that we can reduce the odds of it occurring.

entry.title: WHO chief says Ebola outbreak in Congo is 'spreading rapidly' and upgrades risk assessment
entry.summary: The head of the World Health Organization says the Ebola outbreak in Congo is spreading rapidly and poses a “very high” risk at the national level. WHO Director-General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus announced the U.N. health agency has revised its assessment of …

entry.title: Weight-loss drugs from Lilly, Novo linked to stalling cancer: report
entry.summary: New studies link GLP-1 drugs like Ozempic and Mounjaro to lower cancer progression and death risk, including breast cancer benefits—learn what the data...

entry.title: Widely criticized keto diet study retracted
entry.summary: Aamulya/iStock A 2025 paper claiming the keto diet does not promote the formation of arterial plaques has been retracted after widespread criticism of the study’s methods and claims. The journal fo…

entry.title: Inside an African lab that helped crack the hantavirus outbreak
entry.summary: (Clarifies status of Istitut Pasteur de Dakar in paragraphs 6, 8-9) By Jessica Donati DAKAR, May 22 (Reuters) - The call for help came in early May: a cruise...

entry.title: Air France flight to US diverted to Montreal due to Ebola travel restrictions
entry.summary: U.S. Customs and Border Protection said an Air France flight bound for Detroit was diverted to Montreal after a passenger from the Congo boarded “in error” amid flight restrictions tied to the Ebola outbreak

entry.title: Measles infections detected at 2 Heber City schools as Utah cases continue to rise
entry.summary: Health officials urge caution as measles infections hit two schools in Heber City.

entry.title: Nearly 1.2 billion people worldwide are living with mental disorders. The number has been growing
entry.summary: Nearly 1.2 billion people had a mental disorder in 2023, a new study has found — reflecting a 95.5% increase since 1990.

entry.title: Air France flight to Detroit diverted to Canada under Ebola restrictions
entry.summary: A passenger from Congo should not have been aboard, authorities said. Under new DHS rules, people who recently visited any of three African countries must enter the U.S. via Dulles.

entry.title: De novo design of miniproteins targeting GPCRs
entry.summary: Nature - De novo design of miniproteins targeting GPCRs

entry.title: Experts Reveal the Symptoms of Hantavirus You Should Watch For
entry.summary: Find out what they are and how to stay safe.

entry.title: GLP-1 drugs may reduce the risk of cancer progressing, study suggests
entry.summary: GLP-1 drugs may be linked to a lower risk of cancer progression, according to new research that will be presented next week at the American Society of Clinical Oncology’s annual meeting.

entry.title: GLP-1s Linked to Lower Risk of Cancer Spread in Four Tumor Types
entry.summary: Accumulating evidence of possible benefit warrants randomized trials, experts say

entry.title: Women Are Arguing Online About This Health Procedure, And We Really, Really Need To Talk
entry.summary: Gen Z women argue that these procedures are barbaric, but millennials and medical experts beg to differ, urging the importance of pap smear screenings.

entry.title: American passenger feels 'betrayed' by federal order to stay in hantavirus quarantine
entry.summary: Two passengers from the cruise ship that had a hantavirus outbreak were blocked by the federal government from leaving the quarantine unit. One says she is being detained against her will.

entry.title: Finding the sleep 'sweet spot' could help you live longer, study suggests
entry.summary: A new study in Nature reveals that people who sleep too little or too long show signs of accelerated biological aging and higher disease risk.

entry.title: You Won’t Believe How Much Exercise It Takes to Get ‘Substantial’ Heart Protection
entry.summary: New research suggests that it might take four times the current recommended level of exercise to significantly lower your odds of cardiovascular disease.

entry.title: Ebola outbreak spirals out of control: how might it have started?
entry.summary: Public-health researcher James Baguma has studied the interactions between bats, which can carry the virus, and people in the region near the latest epidemic.

entry.title: Ebola outbreak live updates: Flight bound for Detroit diverted to Canada after Congolese passenger boarded 'in error,' U.S. officials say
entry.summary: Meanwhile, an American doctor who was flown to Germany for treatment after contracting the virus in the Democratic Republic of Congo is "critically ill," his...

entry.title: Acting head of NIH’s infectious disease institute reported to have stepped down
entry.summary: Jeffery Taubenberger, the acting head of the NIAID, the NIH’s infectious disease institute, is reported to have stepped down.

entry.title: Sleep helps brain clean Alzheimer’s-linked toxins, study says
entry.summary: Smartwatch heart measurement could help monitor risks of insufficient slumber, new review claims

entry.title: ‘Do travel bans even work?’ Answering questions about Ebola and hantavirus
entry.summary: The last few weeks have been a whirlwind of public health threats.

entry.title: Rittenhouse dental clinic may have exposed patients to HIV, hepatitis
entry.summary: A dental clinic in Rittenhouse is closed and under investigation for unsanitary practices. These are the patients who should get tested.

entry.title: Medical Professionals Are Sharing The Dumbest Reasons People Visited The ER, And I’m Convinced They Aren’t Paid NEARLY Enough
entry.summary: "Someone broke their acrylic nail."

entry.title: Study finds deadly hantavirus more widespread in Pacific Northwest rodents than expected
entry.summary: A hantavirus that can cause a deadly respiratory illness in humans may be more common in rodent populations in parts of the Pacific Northwest than previously re

entry.title: Ebola responders say aid cuts by Western nations left them ill-equipped for outbreak
entry.summary: The sweeping aid cuts have left the Democratic Republic of Congo struggling to contain an Ebola outbreak despite its extensive experience with the disease.

entry.title: 10-Cent Pill Could Transform Heart Failure Treatment Worldwide
entry.summary: New research indicates that low-dose digoxin may provide significant benefits for heart failure patients.

entry.title: The numbers behind global mental health and its different disorders
entry.summary: Nearly one in eight people in the world lives with a mental disorder, and one person dies by suicide every 43 seconds.

entry.title: Human inflammatory bowel disease-on-a-chip for modelling disease progression, cancer initiation and sex-specific effects
entry.summary: Epithelial–stromal interactions drive inflammation, fibrosis, immune recruitment and cancer initiation in human IBD colon-on-a-chip devices, and responses are exacerbated by peristalsis-like movements and pregnancy-associated hormones in female-derived chips.

entry.title: Irisin as a driver of neuroprotection in multiple sclerosis
entry.summary: Exercise is increasingly recognized as a disease-modifying intervention in multiple sclerosis. A new study published in Nature Metabolism shows that the muscle-derived hormone irisin promotes neuronal survival in an inflammation-driven neurodegenerative model.

entry.title: Is All Protein 'Good' Protein? A Dietitian Ranks Them From Most to Least Healthy
entry.summary: Not all protein is created equal. We ranked 9 common protein sources from healthiest to least healthy, plus what makes some a nutritional win and others a hidden health risk.

entry.title: Study Shows Meat Eaters Are More Likely to Live to 100, But There's a Catch
entry.summary: Humans are living longer than ever before.

entry.title: Scientists Finally Identified Why Mosquitoes Target Certain People Far More Than Others
entry.summary: Some people walk outside covered in bites while others beside them escape untouched. Researchers now know why, and the answer is chemical.

entry.title: The exercise hormone irisin has neuroprotective effects in a mouse model of multiple sclerosis
entry.summary: Exercise-mediated neuroprotection in a mouse model of multiple sclerosis is reduced in mice lacking the irisin-encoding gene, Fndc5, and can be recapitulated by peripheral administration of irisin.

entry.title: Scientists Found the Biggest Factor in How Long You’ll Live. There’s Not Much You Can Do About It.
entry.summary: Genetics were thought to play only a secondary role in determining lifespan—turns out, it’s quite the opposite.

entry.title: 7 Medical Tests and Treatments You Might Be Smart to Skip After 65
entry.summary: Some “routine” medical care after 65 hurts more than it helps — and Medicare won’t pay every bill.

entry.title: Gonorrhoea and syphilis hit record levels in Europe, data shows
entry.summary: STIs have surged thanks to record cases and gaps in testing and prevention, a health agency reports.

entry.title: An Early Warning Sign of Alzheimer's May Be Keeping Some Women Up at Night
entry.summary: Catching Alzheimer's early means a better opportunity for it to be studied and for treatments to be tried.

entry.title: A Drugstore Secret for Treating Perimenopause and PMDD?
entry.summary: “Women will go to their doctors and say, ‘I take Allegra and Pepcid and my PMDD symptoms go away. It’s like magic.’’’ But does it really work, and why?

entry.title: Marathons and ultramarathons may be linked to colon cancer. Here’s why.
entry.summary: A small study found a surprisingly high incidence of precancerous polyps in young, extreme runners, sparking controversy and concern.

entry.title: COVID is shaping Americans' reaction to Ebola and hantavirus
entry.summary: Some Americans seem to be extra-alarmed about Ebola and hantavirus in the wake of COVID-19. But public health experts say they don't expect another pandemic this time.

entry.title: What Ebola and hantavirus are revealing about America’s public health system
entry.summary: Public health experts say neither virus poses an immediate threat to most Americans. But the trust deficit the viruses are exposing could be catastrophic when crisis arrives.

entry.title: Researchers Discover the Body’s Hidden “Off Switch” for Inflammation
entry.summary: Scientists have uncovered a previously underexplored mechanism that may act as one of the body’s natural “off switches” for inflammation.

entry.title: Scientists Discover Metformin Doesn’t Work the Way We Thought
entry.summary: Scientists have uncovered evidence that metformin’s effects may begin in an unexpected place: the intestine. For years, researchers believed metformin, the most widely used medication for type 2 diabetes, worked mainly by reducing glucose production in the li…

entry.title: An Early Clue to Alzheimer's May Appear as Young as 45, Study Finds
entry.summary: Alzheimer's disease, a progressive neurodegenerative disorder that affects millions worldwide, has a long preclinical stage.

entry.title: These Popular Supplements Are Sold With Anti-Aging Claims. Here's What Science Says.
entry.summary: As more people look for ways to stay younger for longer, the supplement industry has moved beyond creams and cosmetic fixes to something more ambitious: products that claim to slow aging by acting on cellular processes.

entry.title: 4 Things to Do Before 10 A.M. to Reduce Dementia Risk
entry.summary: Some morning habits may help reduce dementia risk. Learn what brain experts recommend doing before 10 a.m. for cognitive health.
